sloka_742.meaning: for the brāhmaṇ who is in full knowledge that devotion to the Lord is the sole purport of the Vedas
by a large expanse of water, a lake
by the prayers described in all the Vedas for worshipping the various demigods

sloka_712.meaning: material contact – the engagement of the senses with their objects
produces cold and heat, pleasure and pain sensations

sloka_737.meaning: O Arjuna, descendant of the Kuru dynasty
of the irresolute – those who nurture mundane desires

sloka_702.meaning: O Madhusūdan, slayer of the enemy

sloka_713.meaning: actions of the senses engaging with material sense objects

sloka_710.meaning: It is not a fact that

sloka_736.meaning: from the great fear of birth and death in the material world

sloka_766.meaning: it remains steady, its water never crossing the shore
enter to be enjoyed (for they cannot disturb his mind)

sloka_743.meaning: is to perform your prescribed duties
motivated by the fruits of action

sloka_700.meaning: it is an obstacle to the attainment of heaven

sloka_763.meaning: of that person bereft of self-control

sloka_768.meaning: even at the time of death
liberation from matter and entrance into the divine plane
this is the state of realising Brahma, the Absolute
is freed from the delusion of mundane existence

sloka_716.meaning: and immeasurable, due to his extremely subtle nature

sloka_714.meaning: Such a conclusion, i.e., of the nature
by the seers of the truth

sloka_752.meaning: and whose hankering for pleasure has gone
who is free from attachment, fear, and anger

sloka_1330.meaning: it is inappropriate to grieve for it
imperceptible due to its extremely subtle nature

sloka_727.meaning: for a kṣatriya, or one who upholds the law

sloka_1331.meaning: birth, for undergoing the reactions to his past actions
and for one who is dead

sloka_728.meaning: as an open door to heaven

sloka_703.meaning: by killing our teachers and superiors

sloka_724.meaning: and the situation after death is again unknown
the situation before birth is unknown
so what cause is there for grief
the period between birth and death is known

sloka_722.meaning: reaching all planes, migratory through all species of life according to its actions

sloka_718.meaning: even if the body is destroyed

sloka_762.meaning: For such a person devoid of this intelligence
meditation on the Lord, purity of thought

sloka_699.meaning: his eyes brimming with tears, showing his distress
whose heart was filled with pity

sloka_731.meaning: have been the object of great honour
you will be held the object of ridicule
Great warriors like Duryodhan and others

sloka_739.meaning: and lead to enjoyment and opulence
and they advocate the various ceremonies and rituals
Their hearts filled with selfish desires

sloka_740.meaning: Of those persons attached to enjoyment and opulence
in dedication to the Supreme Lord
by those flowery words of the Vedas

sloka_745.meaning: Those motivated by the fruits of their actions

sloka_759.meaning: falls into saṁsāra, the cycle of birth and death in the material world

sloka_767.meaning: free from the false ego of ‘I’ and ‘mine’ in relation to the body and its attachments (having a divine relationship with the Supreme)

sloka_711.meaning: the attainment of yet another body

sloka_755.meaning: but not the taste to enjoy them

sloka_705.meaning: bewildered in determining my duty and non-duty
Losing heart, thus losing the courage of the warrior by apprehending the fall of the dynasty
ready for Your counsel and direction

sloka_704.meaning: we do not wish to even live

sloka_707.meaning: sense-controlled Arjuna, the conqueror of sleep

sloka_747.meaning: and attaining liberation from the bondage of birth

sloka_734.meaning: By seeing with an attitude of equanimity

sloka_765.meaning: remain awake (experience the pleasures and pains, sorrows and delusions of worldly life)
who sees (the futility of mundane endeavours and remains indifferent to the dualities)
That which is night for all living beings (who, immersed in ignorance, remain asleep to spiritual knowledge)

sloka_750.meaning: How does he be and act
one in samādhi, absorbed in thought of the Supreme
How does such a person of steady mind speak
of a person of firmly established wisdom

sloka_754.meaning: like the limbs of a tortoise

sloka_757.meaning: the person absorbed in consciousness of Me, devoted to Me

sloka_741.meaning: Give up duality (honour and dishonour, etc., in the purview of these modes)
transcendental to the three modes of material nature
be always situated in the truth (or remain with the true devotees)
deal with the modes of material nature – worldly subjects
engaging in this buddhi-yoga, yoga of wisdom that I teach you

sloka_749.meaning: steadily absorbed in consciousness of the Supreme
by the apparent contradictions of the Vedas

sloka_738.meaning: momentarily please the ear (which, thus taken out of context, are effectively poisonous as they do not help the spiritual progress of the soul)
who are fond of interpreting the Vedas, not knowing the true directive of the Vedas

sloka_760.meaning: free from attraction and repulsion, adopting yukta-vairāgya or devotionally integrated detachment
the person who can control his mind at will – whose mind is obedient to him
